title: Royle's Question on G(n)/G(n-1)
statement_text: Does the ratio $G(n)/G(n-1)$ tend to $4$ as $n \to \infty$?
context_text: Royle asked (equivalently phrased via zero-free degree sequences) whether $G(n)/G(n-1) \to 4$. The authors note this is equivalent to asking whether $G'(n)/G'(n-1) \to 4$, where $G'(n)$ counts degree sequences of graphs without isolated vertices, since $G'(n) = G(n-1)$ by a simple observation.
paper_contribution: Answers the question in the affirmative as an immediate corollary of the main result $G(n) = (c_{\deg}+o(1))4^n/n^{3/4}$.
notes: Reference [37] in the paper; year not determinable from the provided text.
paper_title: Counting graphic sequences via integrated random walks
